searchLedger.hypothesis: Normal-path sentinel write precedes shard rename

searchActions.result: The call site computes quarantinePath at line 589, synchronously calls write_vector_shard_repair_pending_sentinel at line 590, and only then calls quarantine_vector_shard at line 591.

searchLedger.rationale: There is no normal-path move-before-sentinel ordering bug; the remaining durability issue is that the write helper catches failures and returns.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: The write-then-act order is explicit and synchronous.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Temp+rename discipline matches corruption sentinel writer

searchActions.result: The repair sentinel temp path is sentinelPath plus .tmp.<pid> at line 1032, so it is in the same directory as the final sentinel. The writer writes the temp file, fsyncs it, renames it, and fsyncs the directory at lines 1033-1049. The corruption sentinel writer uses the same pattern at lines 1141-1150.

searchLedger.rationale: No cross-device rename or torn-write deviation was found relative to the existing proven corruption-sentinel pattern.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Sentinel path is confined to checkpoints and basename-derived

searchActions.result: get_vector_shard_repair_pending_sentinel_path derives the base directory from the vector shard directory at lines 1010-1011 and places .<basename>.repair-pending under checkpoints at lines 1012-1016. The shard filename itself is context-vectors__<profile.slug>.sqlite at line 414.

searchLedger.rationale: Using path.basename prevents path traversal through shardPath, and the profile slug in the shard filename prevents an observed cross-profile collision in this path.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: No path traversal or cross-shard collision evidence in the reviewed commit.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Missing live shard with sentinel does not attach the quarantined shard

searchActions.result: When the live shard path is missing, run_vector_shard_integrity_probe_at_path returns ok at 507-508. With a pending sentinel, attachActiveVectorShard resumes repair at 1410-1411 and then ATTACHes the canonical shardPath at 1414-1415, not sentinel.quarantinePath.

searchLedger.rationale: For the normal quarantine-then-restart case, the corrupt file is at quarantinePath and the canonical shardPath is missing. The reviewed path opens the canonical path, creating/using an empty rebuilding shard, and does not attach the quarantined file.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: The code never uses sentinel.quarantinePath as the attach target.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Degraded seed and completion clear use matching shard basename on the single-shard path

searchActions.result: recordVectorShardQuarantined stores lastShard as basenameOrNull(input.shardPath) at 230. recordVectorShardRebuildCompleted computes completedShard the same way at 246 and clears activeJobId when degradedVectorHealth.lastShard === completedShard at 250-252.

searchLedger.rationale: For the reviewed boot-resume path, resume and completion both use the same canonical shardPath, so the later clear can match the degraded seed.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: No degraded-key mismatch found for the normal single active shard/profile path.

searchLedger.hypothesis: temp database/socket sandbox avoids live shard paths

searchActions.result: beforeEach creates a per-test os.tmpdir root at line 126, sets MEMORY_DB_PATH and MEMORY_ALLOWED_PATHS to that root at lines 128-129, and redirects SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR into the same temp tree at line 131. Each shard path is asserted to start with tempDir and not contain /mcp_server/database/ before destructive/copy operations at lines 171-175, 229-234, and 274-276, and afterEach closes handles and removes tempDir at lines 137-154.

searchLedger.rationale: No reviewed test path reads, writes, renames, or deletes under mcp_server/database/** or a production checkpoint directory.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: All destructive shard operations are derived from activeProfile.getVectorShardPath(tempDir) or dbPath under tempDir, and host socket path is sandboxed before handler use.

searchLedger.hypothesis: restart case is not merely a second attach on the same handle

searchActions.result: The test detaches, writes/moves the sentinel fixture, calls closeDb, resets degraded observability at lines 234-238, then opens unmanagedDb = new Database(dbPath) and attaches with that new handle at lines 248-250.

searchLedger.rationale: This rules out the narrow concern that the test only calls attach once on an already-open store; the sentinel read path is exercised after closing the singleton DB handle.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: It is still in-process rather than a module/process reload, but the test does not rely on an in-memory repair flag because it fabricates only the on-disk sentinel and resets observability before the new attach.

searchLedger.hypothesis: no banned ephemeral labels found in introduced code comments

searchActions.result: The added sentinel/restart/reindex comments do not include spec paths, packet/phase numbers, ADR/REQ/TASK ids, or similar labels. Existing comments such as HIGH-004/C1/BUG-012 appear outside the reviewed diff and were not introduced by commit 1ee9e1e767.

searchLedger.rationale: No hygiene hard-block found in the changed code/comments under review.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Commit diff for the reviewed files shows no newly added banned labels in code comments.
